macro_segments.title: The Principle of Min B'Mino in Tumah: Does Nullification Remove Status Entirely or Just Make it Inaccessible?

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Debate: The Principle of Min B'Mino and When Tumah Disappears
Case of Parah Adumah Ashes Mixed with Regular Ashes: Majority Rule Determines Tumah Status
Gemara's Reframing: If Tumah Reawakens, It Must Be Present and Active, Not Dormant
Rabbi Yose b'Rabbi Chanina's Resolution: Tumah is Present But Inaccessible—Touching Won't Cause Impurity, But Carrying Will
Discussion of Lach B'Lach (Liquids in Liquids) and Solid Mixtures: How Definition Affects Accessibility
Testing the Theory with Neveilah: If Tumah Remains Active, Why No Impurity from Carrying?
The Case of Dead Fetus Mixed with Blood: Distinction Between Bittul and Unfitness for Consumption

macro_segments.title: Bar Padda vs. Rabbi Yochanan: Does Tumah Require Fitness for Human Consumption, Animal Consumption, or Both?

micro_segments.title: Bar Padda's Position: A Source of Tumah (Neveilah) Must Be Fit for Human Consumption
Rabbi Yochanan's Position: Either Type of Tumah Persists Until Completely Unfit Even for Animals
The Stickiness Principle: Once Status is Acquired, It Is Harder to Lose Than to Initially Avoid

macro_segments.title: The Case of Reawakening in Fish Brine: When a Drop of Water Tips the Scales Back to Majority

micro_segments.title: Gemara's Analysis: Does One Drop Make the Difference, and How Much Dilution Do We Assume?

macro_segments.title: Bar Padda vs. Rabbi Yochanan: Does Tumah Require Fitness for Human Consumption, Animal Consumption, or Both? (continued)

micro_segments.title: Application to the Fetus: Was It Ever Fit for Consumption, and Does That Determine Its Status?

macro_segments.title: The Case of Reawakening in Fish Brine: When a Drop of Water Tips the Scales Back to Majority (continued)

micro_segments.title: Conclusion: Double Derabbanan Status (Am Ha'aretz Tumah + Liquid Tumah) Creates Weaker Impurity
The Tzir Tahor Case: Pure Fish Brine Where Water Was Added, Then Water Falls Back In

macro_segments.title: Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel on Nursing Animals: Lactation as Proof of Childbirth and Exemption from Bechor

micro_segments.title: Case One: Buying a Nursing Animal from a Non-Jew—No Concern It Nursed Another's Young
Case Two: Animals in Your Own Flock Giving Birth—Assumption That Each Nurses Its Own Young
Lactation as Evidence: The Mishna Assumes Lactating Animals Have Definitely Given Birth

macro_segments.title: Halachic Ruling: Why Rav's General Statement About Non-Debated Mishnayot Applies Specifically to Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel

micro_segments.title: Rav Nachman's Statement: We Follow All Non-Debated Positions in Our Perek
Rav Sheshet's Question: Which Non-Debated Case Is Being Referred To?
Cases Eliminated: Rabbi Eliezer ben Yaakov Is Undisputed; Rabbi Yossi ben Meshullam Already Stated Separately
Rava's Resolution: The Statement Refers to Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel, Not Debated in the Mishna Despite Braita Dispute
Principle: A Position Not Debated in the Mishna Is Followed Even If Debated in a Braita

macro_segments.title: Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel on Nursing Animals: Lactation as Proof of Childbirth and Exemption from Bechor (continued)

micro_segments.title: Tannaitic Opposition: Braita Suggests a Lactating Animal Might Never Have Given Birth If It Has Compassion
